name: The MMA District
subType: MMA + BJJ (no-gi) + Muay Thai + S&C in Downtown LA
positioning: A boutique, female-founded fight gym in Chinatown / Lincoln Heights, just north of DTLA. Small classes, real fight credentials, multi-discipline curriculum, with a serious-but-personal training culture. Distinct from big-box gyms and from frat-bro MMA gyms.
founderStory: Founded by Hanny Tjan, a 15+ year martial artist trained in Sanda, Muay Thai, Jiu Jitsu, and MMA. Built to combine the intensity of authentic Thai camps with the personal connection of a small community gym.
neighborhood: Chinatown / Lincoln Heights border (N Main St, north of DTLA)

home.metaTitle: MMA District | Where the Fighters Are Made — Downtown LA
home.metaDescription: Boutique MMA, Muay Thai, Jiu-Jitsu, and Strength & Conditioning in Downtown Los Angeles. Founded by lifelong martial artist Hanny Tjan. Small classes, real coaches.
home.heroSubhead: A boutique fight gym in Chinatown / Lincoln Heights, just north of DTLA. MMA, Muay Thai, No-Gi Jiu-Jitsu, and Strength & Conditioning — taught by real fighters, in real classes, at real intensity.

valuePillars.title: Real Training
valuePillars.body: Every session builds fight-ready skills, conditioning, and the mental toughness to perform under pressure. No fluff. No watered-down classes.

valuePillars.title: Real Coaches
valuePillars.body: Brown and purple belt grapplers, championship Muay Thai title holders, 25-year veterans. Credentials on the floor, not just on the wall.

valuePillars.title: Real Community
valuePillars.body: Small classes, personal coaching, and a culture built on sweat, respect, and showing up. Boutique — not big box.

home.philosophyHeadline: Train Like a Fighter. Even If You're Not One.
home.philosophyBody: MMA District is open to anyone willing to put in the work — kids 10+, hobbyists, first-timers, and serious competitors. Beginners build a strong foundation. Advanced athletes refine their edge. Every program is designed to push limits and unlock potential.

about.metaTitle: About | MMA District — Boutique Fight Gym in Los Angeles
about.metaDescription: Founded by lifelong martial artist Hanny Tjan, MMA District is a boutique fight gym blending Thai camp intensity with community gym warmth.
about.heroHeadline: Built by a Fighter. For Fighters.
about.heroSubhead: MMA District was born from one martial artist's vision: combine the intensity of a Thai camp with the personal connection of a community gym.
about.founderBio: MMA District was born out of passion — the kind that only comes from years on the mats, gloves on, and the drive to keep learning. Founder Hanny Tjan has trained in martial arts for over 15 years, from Sanda and Muay Thai to Jiu-Jitsu and MMA. She dreamed of creating a space that combined the intensity of Thai camps with the personal connection of a small community gym. That dream became the District — a boutique training center built for fighters, hobbyists, and kids alike.
about.philosophyHeadline: Train Like a Fighter. Live Like One.
about.philosophyBody: At MMA District, we believe everyone can train like a fighter — not just in skill, but in mindset. Every class is designed to challenge, empower, and teach resilience. Our training blends modern fight preparation with traditional values: discipline, respect, and growth. Whether you're rolling, striking, or lifting, you'll learn to move with confidence and purpose.
about.differentiatorHeadline: Not a Big-Box Gym. Not a Hobby Studio.
about.differentiatorBody: We're a close-knit community where every member matters. Our classes are smaller, our coaching is personal, and our culture is built around accountability. We offer a full range of programs — MMA, Muay Thai, Jiu-Jitsu, and Strength & Conditioning — all in one place. It's the one-stop shop for fighters that Hanny Tjan always wanted to create: a home where anyone with heart and dedication can find their fight.

tenets.label: Boutique
tenets.body: Small classes, personal coaching, real attention.

tenets.label: Multi-Discipline
tenets.body: MMA, Muay Thai, BJJ, S&C — under one roof.

tenets.label: All Levels
tenets.body: Beginners through competitors. Kids 8+ to adult lifers.

tenets.label: Respect-First Culture
tenets.body: Tap early. Communicate often. Train hard, train smart.

classes.metaTitle: Classes | MMA, Muay Thai, Jiu-Jitsu, S&C — MMA District
classes.metaDescription: Full curriculum at MMA District: MMA, Muay Thai, No-Gi Jiu-Jitsu, and Strength & Conditioning. Boutique class sizes, multi-level instruction.
classes.heroSubhead: Every membership gives you access to the complete fight curriculum, plus a youth program for ages 8–15. Train one art, or weave them together — the choice is yours.

programs.name: MMA
programs.subtitle: The Complete Combat System
programs.summary: Striking and Jiu-Jitsu blended into one discipline. Smart, technical, repeatable.

programs.name: Muay Thai
programs.subtitle: The Art of Eight Limbs
programs.summary: Punches, kicks, knees, elbows — balance, timing, and ring IQ. Authentic, technical, scalable.

programs.name: Jiu-Jitsu (No-Gi)
programs.subtitle: Leverage, Timing, Control
programs.summary: No-gi BJJ built for real-world application. Positions, escapes, high-percentage submissions.

programs.name: Strength & Conditioning

programs.summary: Purpose-built S&C for fighters and everyday athletes. Scalable, periodized, no fluff.

classes-mma.metaTitle: MMA Classes | MMA District Los Angeles
classes-mma.metaDescription: Train Mixed Martial Arts in Downtown LA. Striking, no-gi grappling — one complete system at MMA District.
classes-mma.whyBlock: Mixed Martial Arts is the most complete combat system, blending striking and Jiu-Jitsu into one discipline. Our program teaches you how to enter safely, change levels, control positions, and finish with efficiency. Whether you're training for fitness, self-defense, or competition, the approach stays the same: smart, technical, and repeatable.
whatYoullLearn: Striking to grappling transitions: jabs to shots, kicks to clinch
Takedowns and cage work: level changes, entries, finishes, wall rounds
Top control and pressure: passing, ride positions, ground-and-pound mechanics
Bottom options: frames, stand-ups, reversals, and submissions
Fight IQ: pacing, round strategy, and decision-making under pressure
classes-mma.classFlow: Each session follows a consistent structure: warm-up, technical drills, situational rounds, and optional controlled sparring. Drills are progression-based so beginners build safely while advanced students stay challenged.
classes-mma.forBeginners: No experience needed. You'll start with stance, guard, safe entries, and basic takedowns, then move into control and finishing sequences. Coaching is hands-on, intensity is scaled to your level, and partners are selected to make every session productive and safe.
classes-mma.forCompetitors: For athletes preparing to compete, we focus on conditioning, game planning, wall work, and live problem-solving. Expect scenario rounds, time-based pacing, and detailed feedback to sharpen execution.
classes-mma.safety: We train hard while keeping respect and safety first. Bring a mouthguard, 7–10 oz MMA gloves for contact rounds, and shin guards for striking. Communication is constant, and sparring is always optional, supervised, and scaled to your experience level.
classes-mma.whatToBring: Mouthguard, MMA gloves (7–10 oz), shin guards, water, and a towel. Rash guard and shorts without zippers. A courtesy shower is available.

classes-muay-thai.metaTitle: Muay Thai Classes | MMA District Los Angeles
classes-muay-thai.metaDescription: Train authentic Muay Thai in Downtown LA. Build timing, footwork, clinch control, and striking power.
classes-muay-thai.whyBlock: Known as the Art of Eight Limbs, Muay Thai blends punches, kicks, knees, and elbows with balance, timing, and ring IQ. Our program builds real striking fundamentals for self-defense, fitness, and competition. No ego, just smart, technical work that scales to your level.
whatYoullLearn: Footwork and stance: rhythm, distance, and safe exits
Striking fundamentals: jab–cross mechanics, kick technique, knees and elbows
Defense: shields, checks, slips, parries, counters
Clinch work: posture, frames, off-balancing, sweeps, and knees
Padwork and combinations: clean technique under fatigue
Fight IQ: pacing, ring control, and decision-making
classes-muay-thai.classFlow: Sessions follow a clear structure with a dynamic warm-up, technical instruction, padwork or partner drills, and optional controlled sparring. Coaching is hands-on and intensity is set to your experience.
classes-muay-thai.forBeginners: Beginners welcome. No experience required. Build clean technique from the ground up with patient, technical coaching.
classes-muay-thai.clinchAndConditioning: Clinch work and fight-paced conditioning are woven into every block — building the timing and endurance that separates trained strikers from the rest.
classes-muay-thai.safety: Mouthguard required for sparring. Shin guards, 12–16 oz gloves, and hand wraps for full contact rounds. We scale intensity to your level.
classes-muay-thai.whatToBring: Hand wraps, mouthguard, gloves (we can loan if needed for your first class). Shorts, t-shirt or rash guard.

classes-jiu-jitsu.metaDescription: Train No-Gi Jiu-Jitsu at MMA District. Learn control, escapes, submissions, and competition strategy in a safe, technical, welcoming environment.
classes-jiu-jitsu.whyBlock: Jiu-Jitsu teaches leverage, timing, and control — not just strength. Our no-gi program builds practical skills you can rely on: how to stay safe, create escapes, and finish with clean technique. Whether you're here for fitness, self-defense, or competition, we'll help you progress with clarity and confidence.
whatYoullLearn: Positional control: guard, half guard, side control, mount, back control
Escapes and reversals from bad positions
High-percentage submissions: rear naked choke, triangles, armbars, guillotines
Modern no-gi systems: back attacks, leg entanglements, front headlock series
Transitions for MMA: base, posture, and cage awareness
classes-jiu-jitsu.classFlow: Each session follows a simple, effective structure: warm-up, technical instruction, drilling, and optional live rounds. We keep groups small whenever possible.
classes-jiu-jitsu.whoItsFor: Anyone ready to learn and work hard. Beginners start with fundamentals and clear safety standards. Hobbyists get structured drills and rounds. Competitors dive deeper into strategy, pacing, and problem-solving under pressure.
classes-jiu-jitsu.safety: We train hard and smart. Tap early, communicate often, and build at your own pace. Coaches emphasize clean technique and controlled intensity so you improve without unnecessary wear and tear.
classes-jiu-jitsu.competitionPath: Ready to test yourself? We'll help you develop a complete game: entries, control, pacing, and match strategy. From in-house rounds to local tournaments.
classes-jiu-jitsu.whatToBring: No-gi only. Wear a clean t-shirt or rash guard with shorts or spats (no zippers). Flip-flops off the mats. Bring water.

classes-strength-conditioning.metaTitle: Strength & Conditioning | MMA District Los Angeles
classes-strength-conditioning.metaDescription: Purpose-built strength and conditioning for fighters and everyday athletes. Power, endurance, and resilience at MMA District.
classes-strength-conditioning.whyBlock: Technique thrives when your body can keep up. Our Strength and Conditioning program builds the power, endurance, and durability needed to perform on the mats and in life. Sessions are scalable and purposeful — no fluff, just results that carry into MMA, Muay Thai, and Jiu-Jitsu.
howWeTrain: Strength: compound lifts, unilateral balance, posterior chain focus
Power: jumps, medicine ball work, ballistic kettlebell patterns
Energy Systems: aerobic base, threshold intervals, fight-paced circuits
Mobility: hips, T-spine, shoulders — for better positions and safer rounds
Core and Bracing: anti-rotation, carries, grappling-specific stability
classes-strength-conditioning.progression: We start with simple movement and capacity checks, then follow a clear track: learn the pattern, own the pattern, load the pattern. Clear cues, smart rep schemes, steady progress without burnout.
classes-strength-conditioning.forFighters: Competing athletes align lifting with camp phases — accumulation, intensification, taper. Training for life focuses on strong fundamentals and conditioning that makes everyday tasks feel easier.
classes-strength-conditioning.recovery: Durability is a skill. Prep for common hot spots — knees, low back, shoulders — plus cooldown protocols and off-day mobility options.
classes-strength-conditioning.classFlow: Each session: dynamic prep → main strength block → power or carry work → conditioning finisher → mobility/recovery.
classes-strength-conditioning.whatToBring: Athletic wear, water, towel. If you use wrist wraps, knee sleeves, or a belt, bring them.

classes-calendar.metaTitle: Class Schedule | MMA District Los Angeles
classes-calendar.metaDescription: Live class schedule at MMA District. View today's MMA, Striking, Cage Work, and No-Gi sessions. Book through Zen Planner.
classes-calendar.intro: Use the live calendar to see MMA, Striking, Cage Work, and No-Gi class times. Tap any class to view details and reserve your spot.
classes-calendar.bookingNote: New here? Choose a beginner-friendly class or message us — our coaches will point you to the right session. Arrive 10 minutes early for a quick check-in and warm-up.
classes-calendar.hoursNote: Monday through Saturday. Schedule varies by class.

instructors.metaDescription: Meet the coaching team at MMA District: BJJ Brown Belt, IKF Muay Thai Champion, 25-year veteran, and active competitors.
instructors.heroSubhead: The team behind every session. Brown belts, championship title holders, competitors, and lifelong martial artists.

team.name: Hanny Tjan
team.bio: Founder and lifelong martial artist. Trained for 15+ years across Sanda, Muay Thai, Jiu-Jitsu, and MMA. Built MMA District to combine the intensity of authentic Thai camps with the personal connection of a small community gym.

team.name: Jamie Glazier
team.bio: A well-rounded martial artist, high-level self-defense and Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu coach with a brown belt in BJJ, specializing in conditioning for fighters. Combines dynamic bodyweight flow with cross-training to enhance mobility, endurance, and fight performance. Jiu-Jitsu roots from Brazil, competing and training internationally for over 20 years.

team.name: Andres Fajardo
team.bio: Dedicated MMA, Muay Thai, and Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu fighter, and strength and conditioning coach. A purple belt in BJJ, he blends grappling, striking, and athletic performance. Andres designs personalized programs that balance skill development, conditioning, and injury prevention.

team.name: Edward Chan
team.bio: Skilled Muay Thai and MMA fighter with an 8-5 Muay Thai record and IKF East Coast Classic Championship title. With eight MMA fights, he's known for his technical striking, discipline, and well-rounded approach to competition.

team.name: Dawn North
team.bio: With over 25 years of experience, Dawn began training at age 10 and started coaching by 17. She has competed and trained nationwide, working out of top gyms in Florida, Colorado, and California. Specializing in boxing, grappling, and kickboxing, Dawn has also served as Director of Girls Empowerment and Self-Defense in Los Angeles schools.

team.name: Andrew Gamboa
team.bio: MMA and Muay Thai coach based in Los Angeles, actively competing as an amateur Muay Thai and MMA fighter. With over four years of dedicated training in striking and grappling, Andrew focuses on developing strong fundamentals and helping students grow with confidence.

memberships.metaTitle: Memberships & Pricing | MMA District Los Angeles
memberships.metaDescription: Flexible memberships, drop-ins, and discounts for long-term fighters. Train MMA, Muay Thai, Jiu-Jitsu, and S&C in Downtown LA.
memberships.heroSubhead: Pick the path that fits your goals.
memberships.intro: MMA District offers flexible memberships for every fighter — from first-time students to seasoned competitors. Every membership includes full access to MMA, Muay Thai, Jiu-Jitsu, and Strength & Conditioning classes.

tiers.blurb: Full access to all classes. Cancel anytime.

tiers.name: Kids — Month to Month

memberships.howToStart: Text us at (323) 990-4494, fill out the contact form, or sign up online. Our team will set up your membership and help you book your first class.

memberships-fight-fit-transformation.metaDescription: Join the 6-Week Fight Fit Transformation at MMA District. Unlimited classes, free T-shirt, nutrition guide, weekly accountability check-ins.
memberships-fight-fit-transformation.heroSubhead: Train Like a Fighter. See Real Results in 42 Days.
memberships-fight-fit-transformation.intro: Our 6-Week Fight Fit Transformation is built to push your limits and produce real results in a short window. Train consistently, build strength, improve conditioning, and develop real martial arts skills — with the structure and support to make it stick.

whatsIncluded.label: Unlimited Classes
whatsIncluded.body: Full access to every program — MMA, Muay Thai, Jiu-Jitsu, and S&C — for the entire six weeks.

whatsIncluded.label: Free MMA District T-Shirt
whatsIncluded.body: Wear it on day one. Earn it the next 41.

whatsIncluded.label: Nutrition Structure Guide
whatsIncluded.body: Clear, practical guidance to support training and recovery — no extreme cuts, no gimmicks.

whatsIncluded.label: Weekly Accountability Check-Ins
whatsIncluded.body: Stay focused and progressing. Coaches keep you honest with weekly progress reviews.

memberships-fight-fit-transformation.whoItsFor: Anyone ready to challenge themselves. Some join to lose weight and build confidence. Others want to test what it feels like to train like a fighter. No experience required.
outcomes: Earn confidence that lasts beyond the program

memberships-active-duty.metaTitle: Law Enforcement & First Responders Membership | MMA District Los Angeles
memberships-active-duty.metaDescription: Exclusive membership pricing for law enforcement and first responders at MMA District. $180/mo, $0 enrollment, partner free 30 days. Valid department ID required.
memberships-active-duty.heroSubhead: Built for those who show up when it matters.
memberships-active-duty.intro: Your dedication to our community doesn't go unnoticed. As a small token of our gratitude, MMA District offers an exclusive membership rate for those on the front lines.

offer.partnerOffer: Bring a partner free for 30 days

memberships-active-duty.whatToExpect: Train hard, stay ready, and train with people who take discipline seriously. Your membership gives you access to the full class schedule and coaching staff — designed to keep you sharp, conditioned, and progressing.
memberships-active-duty.scarcity: Limited-time enrollment. Spots are limited and reserved for law enforcement and first responders only.

memberships-sign-up.metaTitle: Sign Up | MMA District Los Angeles
memberships-sign-up.metaDescription: Sign up for a membership at MMA District. Quick form, easy onboarding. We'll text you back within the hour.
memberships-sign-up.heroSubhead: Two minutes. We take it from there.
memberships-sign-up.intro: Fill out the form and our team will text you back to confirm your start date and your first class.

contact.heroSubhead: Ready to take the next step?
contact.intro: Whether you're ready to start training, curious about our schedule, or just want to stop by and see the gym — our team is here to help.
contact.address: 1205 N Main St, Los Angeles, CA 90012
contact.businessHours: Monday to Saturday — schedule varies by class
contact.parking: Street parking. Entrance through the parking lot / gate on Elmyra St.
contact.bookingMethod: Text (323) 990-4494 for the fastest reply. Or fill out the form below.

booking.metaTitle: Book Your First Class | MMA District
booking.metaDescription: Reserve your first session at MMA District. Pick your discipline, see the schedule, and lock in your spot.
booking.subhead: You're almost done. Pick a time below.
booking.programNote: Calendar varies by program. Use the program switcher below the calendar if you'd like to see a different discipline.

address.parkingNote: Street parking. Entrance through the parking lot / gate on Elmyra St.

brandColorRoles.primaryNote: Signature burnt-orange/copper from the existing logo badge — the only chromatic accent on the brand. This is THE brand color.
brandColorRoles.successNote: Sage from existing CSS var — keep as a tertiary accent for success/active-duty pricing badges only, never as a main UI color

logoNote: Circular/medallion badge logo with 'MMA DISTRICT' wordmark in burnt-orange copper on black. Strong existing mark — keep visible, do not replace.
designRecommendationNote: Bespoke hybrid: cinematic-fight as the spine (obsidian, oxblood-orange, oversized portrait photography, prestige-TV feel), training-lab for the structured curriculum/credential blocks (data-forward bento, instructor credential tables, S&C metrics), taste-skill typography (editorial display serif paired with utilitarian sans), and a quiet championship-heritage touch in the founder section (because the female-founder story deserves craft and dignity, not bro energy).
currentSiteIssues: Generic 'fighter silhouette' hero — doesn't communicate the boutique female-founded character
Logo badge is good but hero typography is generic sans-serif, no editorial weight
No fighter portraits in hero — instructor credentials are the gym's strongest asset and they're buried 4 clicks deep
Active Duty offer ($180 vs $200, partner free, $0 enrollment) is buried in nav, should be a value-strip on every page
Founder story (Hanny Tjan — female martial artist, 15-year vet) is invisible above the fold; this is the unique positioning
Class pages use thin generic text — there's actually rich content on the site but the design doesn't elevate it
Mobile feels mostly-OK but lacks any kinetic energy / motion / cinematic weight
No real testimonials section, no fight footage, no proof anchoring the 'where fighters are made' claim
Color palette is reading as flat black + white because the burnt-orange brand color is severely underused

logo.note: Circular MMA District badge in burnt-orange on black

hero.description: Single fighter silhouette gripping glove, moody dark backdrop
